The proposed alternative solution is for the elimination of tactile attention indicators around the pool deck of the public pool serving the mixed-use/residential building. OBC Clause 3.11.3.1.(14) requires a public pool be provided with a tactile attention indicator conforming to OBC article 3.8.3.18 between the pool deck and any area contiguous to the pool deck which may be confused with the deck. In consideration of the Functional Statements and Objectives associated with e OBC clause 3.11.3.1.(14), in lieu of providing tactile attention indicator, it is proposed to provide the following design characteristics to achieve sufficiency of compliance with the above OBC requirements: The 1800mm wide pool deck proper will be constructed of a different material than the remainder of the deck in the pool area, the raised wall of the pool will provide a physical boundary between the pool deck and the pool basin, and access into the pool area is fenced such that a person entering the pool area would have indication that a pool is near by.

pdn.desc: Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment Applicaitons to facilitate the redevelopment of the site for a 28-storey mixed-use building having a non-residential gross floor area of 971 square metres, and a residential gross floor area of 24,278.4 square metres.  A total of 351 residential dwelling units and 228 parking spaces in three levels underground are proposed.**Note related Site Plan Control Application 21 251831 NNY 08 SA

The proposed SPA application will facilitate the development of an 8-storey mixed usebuilding containing 57 residential dwelling units totalling 3,966 square metres ofresidential gross floor area and 372 square metres of commercial gross floor area,resulting in a density of 5.29 times the area of the lot. 18 stacked parking spaces for residential use areproposed along with 57 bicycle parking spaces.

The proposed plan consists of a 10 storey residential mixed use mid-rise, with retail at grade. A total of 19,084 m2 residential GFA and 606 m2 non-residential retail GFA are included, along with 1 below grade parking level. 274 residential units are proposed from floors 2-10 and a mechanical penthouse level with indoor and outdoor amenity. The below grade level includes 68 residential stalls, 1 car share stall, 15 retail/visitor stalls and 160 long term bicycle spaces. A Zoning By-Law Amendment application was submitted in March 2023 and has been deemed complete as of May 3, 2023 (23 122250 WET 03 OZ).

The purpose of the Zoning By-law amendment applications is to permit 4 residential and mixed use buildings with heights of 43, 31, 29, and 25 storeys. The applicant is proposing a total of 1,542 residential units and 1,902 parking spaces.  The applicant is proposing the development be built in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 43 and 31 storey buildings and phase 2 consisting of the 29 and 25 storey buildings. The properties subject to the zoning by-law amendment application are 5945-5949 Yonge Street, 5959 Yonge Street, 1 & 2 Doverwood Court, 15 Wedgewood Drive, and 48 Cummer Avenue. This site is also subject to related Official Plan Amendment application (file # 16 156153 NNY 24 OZ) which applies to all of the following properties 5925-5931,  5935A, 5945-5949, 5959, and 5995 Yonge Street, 11, 15, 17, 19, 21, 23, and 25 Wedgewood Drive, 1-8 Doverwood Court, and 42, 44, 46, 48, 50, 52, and 54 Cummer Avenue.

Site Plan Control application for a 41 storey mixed-use residential building, comprised of 521 residential units, 421 square metres of at-grade commercial space and a 300 square metre off-site parkland dedication.

Site Plan Approval application for a mixed-use building having a non-residential gross floor area of 906 square metres, and 43,296 square metres of residential gross floor area. A total of 672 residential units in two towers of 20 and 27 storeys atop a six storey base building. A new public park of 621.5 square metres will be at the southeast corner of the site.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for Phases 4 & 5 of the Regent Park redevelopment. The Phases 4 & 5 lands are generally bounded by Gerrard Street to the north, River Street to the east, Oak Street to the south, and Dreamers Way to the west. The proposal consists of a total gross floor area (GFA) of 320,064 m2 comprised of approximately 303,616 m2 of residential GFA and approximately 16,452 m2 of non-residential GFA across 13 buildings (including a new Toronto Public Library branch relocated from Parliament Street). The revised proposal is to develop a 12-storey (plus Mechanical Penthouse) residential building containing a gross floor area of 15,703.96 sq. m (169,036.02 sq. ft) and a density of 1.11 FSI. This provides for a proposed residential unit count of 285 dwellings, consisting of 80 studio units (28.07%), 113 one-bedroom units (39.65%), 77 two-bedroom units (27.02%), and 15 three-bedroom units (5.26%).There is approximately 570 sq m (6,135 sq ft) of indoor amenity space located on the B2 and ground floor, and 570 sq m (6,135 sq ft) of outdoor amenity space located on the ground floor, for a total amenity area of 1,140 sq m (12,270 sq ft) or a total 4 sq m per unit. Parking is proposed at-grade and contains a total of 50 parking spaces, including 16 visitor parking spaces and 9 accessible spaces. A total of 20 short-term bicycle parking spaces are proposed at-grade and 194 long- term bicycle parking spaces are proposed on the B2 level, for a total of 214 bicycle parking spaces.

Proposed redevelopment of a former KFC restaurant site with a new 7-storey mixed use building containing 19 residential units (GFA of 2,199 m2) and commercial space on the ground floor (93 m2). A total of 18 parking spaces are proposed at-grade, of which 6 spaces will be accessed via parking.  stackers.   NOAC issued April 21, 2020 but Statement of Approval not yet achieved.. NOAC extended Feb 2, 2023 for two more years.   On October 8, 2024 applicant has come in with a revised proposal under the same application.  The contemplated development is 6 storeys and 26 units, and is consistent with the setbacks required by the Zoning by-law with an increase in setback to 13.48 metres from the south lot line abutting the existing residential property. The building floor plate has reduced to an average of 376.35 square meters and thus the overall gross floor area to 1996.34 square metres and a reduction in floor space index to 2.45. Additionally, the number of units has increased to 26 and with the introduction of studio apartments along with 1 bed, 2 bed and 3 bed typologies. The average size of units is 63.75square meters. The commercial area is located at grade with an area of 65.62 square metres and has been repositioned towards Danforth Road ¿ Byng Avenue intersection giving it additional frontage.  The onsite stacked parking is removed, thus reducing one parking space. The bike parking spaces have been increased to 33 long-term spaces in the basement and 7 short-term at grade.

A Development Application has been submitted to amend the Official Plan and Zoning By-law to allow for a long-term, mixed use plan for the entire Yorkdale Shopping Centre site. The original application included three conceptual block master plan options that comprised of a range of retail, office, hotel and residential uses with a maximum proposed total future gross floor area of 682,670 square metres and 2.29 Floor Space Index and an internal private above and below grade street network. The application has been revised into a single concept with expanded parkland and the inclusion of a public road. The proposal continues to propose a range of uses, including affordable housing which is intended to occur in a phased manner over the next 20+ years

Rezoning application to redevelop the existing low-rise commercial building and associated surface parking area with a 33-storey (109 metres, plus mechanical space) mixed use building, with 445-residential units, and 29,009 square metres of residential gross floor area.   The proposal includes 806 square metres of retail space on the ground floor, along Danforth Avenue and St. Dunstan Drive. 1,903 square meters of indoor and outdoor amenity space are also proposed. The proposal comprises of a tower with a five storey podium that wraps the building on three sides. Surface parking and a two-level underground parking garage provide 149 vehicle parking spaces, including  eight spaces at grade. A total of 348 bicycle parking spaces are proposed for the building, with 32 short-term spaces, 304 long term spaces and twelve publicly accessible spaces. Access to the site and the underground parking garage is via a driveway at the north end of the site, which connects the two abutting streets, Macey Avenue and St. Dunstan Drive. Note that Community Planning intends to bring forward this application to Scabrough Community Council on April 30th, 2026 for a decision report.

The proposal is to construct a new 49-storey mixed-use building with three levels of underground parking. The ground level includes a shared residential and office lobby as well as retail and commercial uses fronting onto Danforth Avenue and a portion of Pape Avenue. Office uses are proposed from the third to fifth floors and the residential use is proposed from the sixth to 49th floors. Above the 49th floor is the mechanical penthouse and an indoor and outdoor amenity space. Additional indoor amenity spaces are located on the sixth and seventh floors and an additional outdoor amenity space is proposed on the sixth floor with access to the indoor amenity space. The proposal includes a total of 496 dwelling units, 29,756 sq. m (320,291 sq. ft.) of residential gross floor area (GFA) and 4,743 sq. m (51,053 sq. ft.) of non-residential GFA. The total proposed GFA is 9,997.4 sq. m, resulting in a FSI of 20.63.

This is a Housing Now application, known as Block 1of the Bloor Kipling Block Context Plan. Proposed development mixed-use development is comprised of a 25-storey tower and a 29-storey tower, connected by a base building that varies between seven to 11 storeys. There is a proposed publicly accessible courtyard on site serving as a mid-block connection. The project will deliver 217 new affordable rental units through the Housing Now Initiative.

The proposed development contemplates a 7-storey (25.1 metres) mixed-use building with a total of 2807 square metres of residential GFA and 125 square metres of commercial GFA at grade. 30 residential units are proposed, ranging in size from 1-bedroom units to 3-bedroom units. Separate entrances for the residential and commercial components are included in the front façade. Indoor amenity space is proposed on the 7th storey adjacent to outdoor amenity space.A road widening of 3.5 m is proposed along Danforth Avenue and a road widening of 1.17 m is proposed on the rear laneway. Access to parking is from the rear laneway. A total of 21 parking spaces are proposed, including 18 spaces for residents within a parking stacker system, and 3 spaces for visitors a grade, one of which will be an accessible space. 22 bicycle parking spaces are provided.

The proposed SPA application will facilitate the development of an 8-storey mixed usebuilding containing 57 residential dwelling units totalling 3,966 square metres ofresidential gross floor area and 372 square metres of commercial gross floor area,resulting in a density of 5.29 times the area of the lot. 18 stacked parking spaces for residential use areproposed along with 57 bicycle parking spaces.

The proposal is to construct a new 49-storey mixed-use building with three levels of underground parking. The ground level includes a shared residential and office lobby as well as retail and commercial uses fronting onto Danforth Avenue and a portion of Pape Avenue. Office uses are proposed from the third to fifth floors and the residential use is proposed from the sixth to 49th floors. Above the 49th floor is the mechanical penthouse and an indoor and outdoor amenity space. Additional indoor amenity spaces are located on the sixth and seventh floors and an additional outdoor amenity space is proposed on the sixth floor with access to the indoor amenity space. The proposal includes a total of 496 dwelling units, 29,756 sq. m (320,291 sq. ft.) of residential gross floor area (GFA) and 4,743 sq. m (51,053 sq. ft.) of non-residential GFA.
